title: Bridge Paper: Embodied Predictions, Agency, and Psychosis.
author: Frontier Science / Hermes T2

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Nested Agency** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge thesis:** Free will — sense of agency, voluntary action, intentional binding, volitional control.
**Phase context:** sense of agency, voluntary action, free will, intentional binding, volition

Paper: Embodied Predictions, Agency, and Psychosis.
DOI: 10.3389/fdata.2020.00027
Abstract: Psychotic symptoms, i.e., hallucinations and delusions, involve gross departures from conscious apprehension of consensual reality; respectively, perceiving and believing things that, according to same culture peers, do not obtain. In schizophrenia, those experiences are often related to abnormal sense of control over one's own actions, often expressed as a distorted sense of agency (i.e., passivity symptoms). Cognitive and computational neuroscience have furnished an account of these experience

notes: Auto-acquired from PubMed/MEDLINE on 2026-07-11. Phase 4 (Nested Agency).
